prerequisites.value: Knowledge (history) 3 ranks, must have a connection to a lost culture (see text).

description.value:
**Benefit:** Each lost culture has its own daily ritual, but none of them take more than 1 hour per day to perform. Once youâve performed the daily ritual, you gain the benefit of a special ability or resistance as indicated in the Daily Ritual entry for the culture in question. Specific cultures and their rituals are listed below. If you have at least 12 Hit Dice, you also gain the first boon granted by your lost culture upon undertaking its daily ritual. If you have at least 16 Hit Dice, you also gain the lost cultureâs second boon. If you have 20 Hit Dice or more, you also gain the lost cultureâs third boon. Each boon is a spell-like ability that you can cast once per day. Your caster level for these boons is equal to your Hit Dice, and the saving throw DC is based on your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (whichever is highest). For the purposes of this feat, you are considered to have a connection to a lost culture if you have a relic or object from that civilization, have extensively researched that culture, or are descendedâdirectly or culturallyâfrom that cultureâs people. Once you have chosen a lost culture, you cannot change it without your GMâs permission. At the GMâs discretion, other types of connections might also apply. Typically, you can have a connection to only one lost civilization. If you ever fail to perform a daily ritual, you lose all access to the benefits and boons granted by this feat until the time you next perform the daily ritual. Culture A Daily Ritual: Position yourself where you can watch the sun rise. As it does, briefly give thanks to each god in your culture’s pantheon. Reflect on your notable ancestors or other ancient heroes and their accomplishments. Then, leave a small offering of cooked food on a wooden plate (or, if none is available, on a large leaf ).

You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws to resist death effects. Boons: 1: sepia snake sigil; 2: elemental body IV; 3: mageâs disjunction. Culture B Daily Ritual: Stand tall, with one arm outstretched, clutching a crystal, gem, or ioun stone in your fist. Keeping your arm outstretched, slowly turn three times in a counterclockwise circle, each time naming aloud one of your achievements or strengths.

You gain a +2 bonus on Spellcraft and Use Magic Device checks. Boons: 1: arcane sight; 2: reverse gravity; 3: overwhelming presence. Culture C Daily Ritual: Read sections from the Poleiheira. If you do not have any portions of these journals, you can instead meditate on the nature of civilization, the importance of structure and discipline to society, and the ways in which technology can improve the lives of the people.

You gain a +1 bonus on damage rolls for non-magical masterwork weapons and a +2 bonus on Craft checks to create golems. Boons: 1: keen edge; 2: control construct; 3: wooden phalanx. Culture D Daily Ritual: Carefully write your name in calligraphy in the center of a blank piece of parchment. Then, write around it the names of those to whom you have obligations: your family members, superiors, dependents, business partners, neighbors, and so on, mapping the personal connections in a wheel about yourself. If no paper is available, the writing can be performed in a patch of smooth sand or earth instead.

You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws to resist emotion effects. Boons: 1: tongues; 2: form of the dragon I; 3: resplendent mansion. Culture E Daily Ritual: Recall a battle with a magical beast you participated in that occurred within the last week. Meditate on your technique and identify ways to improve for your next combat. If you have not fought a magical beast recently, instead train for 1 hour, chanting war cries while you do.

You gain a +2 bonus on damage rolls against magical beasts. Boons: 1: monstrous physique I; 2: regenerate; 3: symbol of strife. Culture F Daily Ritual: With your back to the rising sun, travel directly westward (or as close to directly westward as you can manage). At the first
